doctor_command.stdout_tail:

GLASS Doctor
Python: 3.12.10 (Windows-10.0.26200)

Windows package try order: cuda13, cuda12, cuda11, cpu

Recommendation: cuda

local_normalization.cpu: tile median/std baseline with coefficient artifacts
local_normalization.cuda: tile mean/std primitive, resident global/grid mean/std, resident grid apply
local_normalization.status: partial; full interpolated/windowed LN is not complete

registration.cuda: resident translation, catalog similarity, triangle descriptors, bilinear/Lanczos3 matrix warp

weighted_integration.cuda: resident weighted mean, simple_snr weights, and sigma/winsorized rejection maps

devices.name: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Workstation Edition

doctor_json.full_name: GPU-Accelerated Lightframe Alignment and Stacking System

windows_cuda_packages.guidance: Try cuda13 first for native performance; if it fails to load, try cuda12, cuda11, cpu. Newer GPUs may run older CUDA packages through PTX JIT.

packages.audience: Maxwell through Ampere, and newer GPUs as a compatibility fallback
packages.driver_hint: NVIDIA driver new enough for CUDA 11.8
packages.label: cuda11
packages.note: This package is expected to run through CUDA PTX forward JIT when the installed NVIDIA driver supports the bundled CUDA runtime. First launch may be slower and performance can be below a native cubin build.

packages.audience: Turing, Ampere, Ada, Hopper, and newer GPUs as a compatibility fallback
packages.driver_hint: NVIDIA driver new enough for CUDA 12.4
packages.label: cuda12
packages.note: This package is expected to run through CUDA PTX forward JIT when the installed NVIDIA driver supports the bundled CUDA runtime. First launch may be slower and performance can be below a native cubin build.

packages.audience: Recent NVIDIA GPUs, including Blackwell targets
packages.driver_hint: NVIDIA driver new enough for CUDA 13.x
packages.label: cuda13
packages.note: This package contains native GPU code for this compute capability.

selected_device.name: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Workstation Edition

help_command.stdout_tail:
measure selected tile-local apply residuals against a
                        reference image
    tile-local-policy-decision
                        rank and accept/reject measured tile-local policy
                        candidates
    tile-local-policy-sweep
                        rank multiple measured tile-local policy decision
                        artifacts
    tile-local-sweep-plan
                        plan a measured tile-local policy sweep command queue
    speedup-summary     summarize GLASS timing against WBPP black-box timing
    acceptance-audit    verify a real GLASS/WBPP acceptance benchmark from
                        existing artifacts
    release-promotion-decision
                        decide whether release/default promotion evidence is
                        ready or needs controlled repeat benchmarking
    windows-release-matrix
                        audit Windows CPU/CUDA package matrix readiness from
                        doctor and release decision artifacts
    windows-package-build-plan
                        plan Windows CPU/CUDA portable package builds from
                        local Toolkit availability
    windows-package-suite
                        aggregate Windows portable package smoke artifacts
                        into a release-suite readiness report
    windows-release-manifest
                        record Windows release zip sizes and SHA256 checksums
                        from a package suite
    windows-package-smoke
                        smoke-test a Windows portable package folder and
                        optional zip artifact
    resident-determinism
                        compare resident CUDA registration signatures from two
                        GLASS runs
    resident-registration-audit
                        summarize resident CUDA triangle registration
                        candidate and pixel-refine diagnostics
    resident-registration-compare
                        join resident sweep compare results with resident
                        registration candidate audits
    resident-runtime-compare
                        compare resident CUDA timing and I/O pipeline
                        artifacts from GLASS runs
    resident-runtime-repeat-plan
                        plan repeated resident CUDA runs for cache and I/O
                        variance checks
    resident-runtime-repeat-execute
                        execute or dry-run a resident-runtime-repeat-plan
    resident-runtime-repeat-preflight
                        check whether a resident-runtime-repeat-plan is ready
                        to execute
    resident-calibration-contract
                        audit resident CUDA master-calibration evidence from
                        resident_artifacts.json
    resident-result-contract
                        audit resident CUDA integration outputs for result-
                        contract invariants
    resident-registration-triage
                        triage resident registration failures and determinism
                        drift between candidate audits
    stack-engine-contract
                        audit StackEngine default routing and DQ provenance
                        from a GLASS run
    pipeline-contract   audit DQ, LN, rejection, crop, and output-map
                        invariants from a GLASS run
    guardrails          generate StackEngine, pipeline, optional pixel, and
                        HTML guardrail artifacts for a run
    blackbox-package    write a PixInsight/WBPP black-box handoff package
    blackbox-finalize   finalize a PixInsight/WBPP black-box timing package
    blackbox-history    extract user-generated WBPP FastIntegration
                        ProcessingHistory from a XISF master
    synthetic           generate synthetic FITS data

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --version             show program's version number and exit

limitations: This smoke test validates package structure and CLI startup; it does not sign or publish artifacts.
CPU-only package smoke does not prove CUDA native module loading.
A CUDA release package must be smoked with require_cuda=true on the target release machine.
